patches.hypothesis: The current method does not handle non-ASCII characters, leading to incorrect parsing of bytes above 127.
patches.changes: - Convert buffer byte to char using proper charset decoder instead of direct casting
patches.diff: - Convert buffer byte to char using proper charset decoder instead of direct casting

patches.hypothesis: The method incorrectly converts bytes with high values to incorrect characters. The method needs to handle bytes as unsigned values.

The previous patch was overly specific in its handling of character encoding and did not handle null bytes efficiently. The refined fix uses a StringBuilder instead of creating strings prematurely, improves readability by managing indices correctly, and continues supporting all valid ASCII characters, inherently handling non-ASCII characters as expected given the byte array input.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug is caused by the assumption that the buffer only contains standard ASCII characters. Characters with byte values above 0x7F are likely being interpreted incorrectly leading to incorrect parsing results.

patches.hypothesis: The parseName method incorrectly processes sign-extended values for bytes > 127. Casting directly to char without handling byte-to-char conversion may result in unexpected character representations.
patches.changes: - Casting bytes to chars correctly to avoid sign-extension issue when converting bytes > 127
patches.diff: - Casting bytes to chars correctly to avoid sign-extension issue when converting bytes > 127

patches.hypothesis: The issue is caused by the conversion of bytes to characters. Characters should be treated as unsigned bytes.
patches.changes: - append((char)(buffer[i] & 0xFF)) to handle bytes as unsigned
patches.diff: - append((char)(buffer[i] & 0xFF)) to handle bytes as unsigned
